page: Microsoft Azure FX16-4mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, configured with 2 physical cores and 4 vCPUs with 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 336.0 GB of system memory yielding 84.0 GB per core, and 945 GB of local storage. It is premium storage capable and does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing with a score of 2357.0 and text processing at 2814.0, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression at 34.85 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.39 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance at 4437.16 and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression at 28.27 GB/sec falling into the weak category. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is also weak at 36.33 GB/sec, though cached small-block read bandwidth reaches an average of 345.84 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX16-4mds_v2 offers a highly specialized resource density, providing an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio. This makes it a qualitative fit for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and specific text-processing applications. Conversely, the low core count and weak multi-core scaling make it inefficient for highly parallelized compute tasks, multi-threaded compilation, or large-scale LLM inference, where text generation speeds for medium and large models are poor.
description: Microsoft Azure FX16-4mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the FXmdsv2 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 4 vCPUs, a 260 MB L3 cache, 336.0 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage. The server delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by its Geekbench single-core score of 2357.0 and strong single-threaded decompression rates. However, its multi-core capabilities are weak, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 4437.16 and poor large-block uncached memory read bandwidth. This hardware profile provides an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive, single-thread-bound workloads such as in-memory databases and text processing, while remaining less suitable for heavily parallelized compute tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 336 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, offering top-tier single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 vCPUs, 336 GB RAM, 945 GB local storage, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A specialized high memory server offering exceptional single core performance and local storage for memory intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
336.0 GB of RAM providing an exceptionally high 84.0 GB per core
945 GB of local storage with premium storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text-processing benchmarks
Weak multi-core scaling and uncached memory read bandwidth tradeoffs
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and single-threaded workloads
